title: Resource APIs, Pagination, Idempotency, and Concurrency Control · Systems Transfer
summary: An independent service team receives a different API, load, trust, persistence, and failure constraint and must transfer resource apis, pagination, idempotency, and concurrency control into a new stable resource and mutation consistency contract. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of stable resource and mutation consistency contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Define stable transport schemas, domain models, persistence records, optional and null semantics, field ownership, and evolution boundaries separately.
Build opaque cursor pagination from a unique stable order, bound page size, preserve filters and authorization, and test inserts and deletes between pages.
Scope and validate idempotency keys, bind request fingerprints, serialize first execution, persist terminal outcome, expire records, and reject mismatched replay.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Commit to the mission model · Lab Resource Schema Boundary
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab team handles case 3855 in a partner API with an outdated generated client. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate optimistic version conflicts from valid idempotency replay; then saturate one admission budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?
